senses.definition: the act or process of preparing or bringing something into a standard form before formal standardization
examples: The researchers discussed the prestandardization steps before the official standardization protocol.
Prestandardization is often overlooked in the early phases of product development.

senses.definition: to perform actions in advance to bring something to a form suitable for standardization
examples: We need to prestandardize the data before importing it into the database.
The team prestandardizes terminology to ensure consistency.

senses.definition: described or prepared in advance to meet standardization criteria
examples: The prestandardized dataset was ready for metadata tagging.
Prestandardized terms reduced ambiguity in the report.

senses.definition: the act of preparing in advance to bring something into a standard form
examples: We are prestandardizing the data schema before the migration.
Prestandardizing terminology helps across departments.

senses.definition: an obsolete or rare term; historically used to mean a stand-in or visible pretense of presence
examples: In old manuscripts, prestant appears as an unused placeholder.
The term prestant may appear in archival glossaries.

senses.definition: an earlier state of a condition or status before formal establishment
examples: The prestate of the agreement was informal.
We reviewed the prestate to understand bargaining dynamics.

senses.definition: stated or declared in advance
examples: The prestated goals guided the project from the start.
Prestated assumptions shaped the analysis.

senses.definition: the act of stating something in advance
examples: Prestating the objectives helped align the team.
They are prestating the terms for transparency.

senses.definition: a service or act performed, typically in a formal or technical sense
examples: The doctor provided a prestation to the patient.
In some contexts, prestation refers to a performance or service.

senses.definition: plural of prestation; services or acts performed
examples: The charity offered several prestations to the community.
Payment for prestations is due monthly.

senses.definition: relating to or involving statistics that are prepared or used before formal statistical analysis
examples: Prestatistical methods were used to clean the data.
The report notes prestaristical observations that informed the study design.

senses.definition: a stage before full steam or a prefix; used in some technical or historical contexts
examples: The vessel entered presteam as the boiler warmed up.
Presteam monitoring indicated rising pressure.

senses.definition: having been dressed or prepared beforehand, especially in historical contexts
examples: The coat was prested for the ceremony.
Prested garments were common in that era.

senses.definition: a hypothetical or rare term referring to steel prepared before processing; not common in modern usage
examples: Some archival texts mention presteel stages in metalworking.

senses.definition: a person who prests or presters; a rarely used or historical term
examples: The manuscript mentions a prester as a helper in the church.

senses.definition: to sterilize in advance; to prepare by sterilization beforehand
examples: We plan to presterilise the equipment before use.
Presterilise the instruments to prevent contamination.

senses.definition: having had sterilization performed beforehand
examples: Presterilised equipment reduces infection risk.
We used presterilised tools for the procedure.

senses.definition: to sterilize something in advance (third-person singular)
examples: The department presterilises the facilities each week.
She presterilises equipment before experiments.

senses.definition: the act of sterilizing in advance (present participle)
examples: Presterilising the instruments is standard procedure.
They are presterilising all reusable tools.

senses.definition: to sterilize in advance
examples: We must presterilize all equipment before surgery.
The kit is designed to prestérilize rapidly.

senses.definition: sterilized in advance
examples: Presterilized syringes are ready for use.
The presterilized packages must remain sealed.

senses.definition: sterilizes in advance (third-person singular)
examples: The supplier presterilizes equipment for safety.
Presterilizes packaging to prevent contamination.

senses.definition: sterilizing in advance (present participle)
examples: They are presterilizing the instruments for the procedure.
Presterilizing helps reduce infection risk.

senses.definition: an anatomical term referring to the sternum area in front of the chest; used chiefly in medical contexts
examples: The imaging showed a presterna fracture.
Pain near the presterna can indicate chest injury.

senses.definition: situated in front of the breastbone (sternum); relating to the presternum
examples: Presternal muscles were described in the anatomical text.
The presternal area was marked on the diagram.
